title: Moving High-Density BPO Centers: Packing and Inventory Management for 200+ Workstations
excerpt: High-density BPO center move: 200+ workstations packing inventory management, cable labeling, fiber cutover, commercial relocation Addis Ababa.
content:
Moving high-density BPO centers: packing and inventory management for 200+ workstations

Two hundred workstations are two hundred identical failure points—one unlabeled cable equals Monday login riot. High-density BPO moves scale inventory discipline: color rows, photo backs, sealed agent kits, and fiber cutover measured in minutes not hours downtime SLA breach.

This guide packs and inventories BPO centers with 200+ workstations.

Inventory architecture

  • Floor plan grid A1-Z20 seat IDs match asset tags.
  • Photo cluster each desk rear cables.
  • Color zip Row A blue Row B green consistent.
  • Agent kit bag: headset keyboard mouse drawer.
  • Server rack elevation separate IT vendor chain.

Packing waves

Wave 1 inactive seats; Wave 2 active floor half Saturday; Wave 3 remainder Sunday; monitors double-box or original stack max four.

Fiber and phones

ISP parallel; PBX forward; patch panel mirror labels; test login thirty agents sample before all-hands.

Acoustic booths

Modular panels numbered diagram reassembly.

Active Movers BPO 200+ package

Labeling crew dedicated; enclosed truck; weekend scale quote per seat.

Conclusion

200+ seat BPO moves succeed on grid inventory, agent kits, labeled cables, and Sunday login test—high density rewards bureaucracy not speed alone.
author: Active Movers Team
